_comment: Engineering standards reference injected into EngInfoSpecialistAgent and EngDocSpecialistAgent system prompts.

gdt_tolerances_by_process._note: Achievable tolerances (±) per process per ASME Y14.5-2018 and Machinery's Handbook 31st ed.

LASER_CUT.note: ±0.010" typical for fiber laser on mild steel. Tighter possible with slow passes.

TUBE_LASER.note: Tube rotation adds ±0.005" vs flat laser.

WATERJET.note: No HAZ; tighter tolerance possible with low traverse speed.

PRESS_BRAKE.note: Bend angle ±1° typical. Formed height ±0.030". CNC brake improves to ±0.5°.

DRILL.note: Drill press location ±0.020" without jig. Diameter ±0.005" with standard drill.

TAP.note: Location same as DRILL. Tap itself follows drilled hole.

CNC_MILL.note: CNC mill can achieve ±0.001" with care. Standard production ±0.003".

WELD_TIG.note: Weld distortion typically ±0.060" on small parts. Fixture and backstep reduce distortion.

WELD_MIG.note: MIG distortion similar to TIG on structural carbon steel.

material_process_compatibility._note: Flags incompatible or suboptimal material+process pairings per AWS D1.1 and vendor best practice.

6061_ALUMINUM + WELD_MIG.reason: 6061-T6 is highly susceptible to HAZ softening and cracking with MIG. AWS D1.2 requires TIG or specialized MIG wire (ER4043/ER5356). Use WELD_TIG.

5052_ALUMINUM + WELD_MIG.note: 5052 can be MIG welded with ER5356 filler. Acceptable but TIG preferred for critical joints.

304_STAINLESS + WELD_MIG.note: MIG acceptable for 304 SS with ER308L wire. TIG preferred for thin gauges (<0.120") or sanitary applications.

316_STAINLESS + WELD_MIG.note: MIG with ER316L acceptable. TIG preferred for corrosion-critical joints.

304_STAINLESS + ANODIZE.reason: Anodize is an aluminum-only process (electrochemical oxidation of Al2O3). Stainless cannot be anodized. Use PAINT or POWDER_COAT for stainless finish.

A36_STEEL + ANODIZE.reason: Anodize is aluminum-only. Use PAINT, POWDER_COAT, or ZINC_PLATE for steel.

A36_STEEL + CHROMATE.reason: Chromate conversion (MIL-DTL-5541) is aluminum-only. For steel corrosion protection use ZINC_PLATE or PAINT.

A36_STEEL + TUBE_LASER.reason: TUBE_LASER requires tubular stock (HSS/DOM/ERW). A36 is plate/bar stock. Use LASER_CUT for A36 flat profiles.

round_bar + PRESS_BRAKE.reason: Round bar cannot be press-brake bent — no flat surface for back-gauge. Use LATHE or CNC_MILL for round bar forming.

tube + PRESS_BRAKE.reason: HSS/tube cannot be press-brake bent (collapses). Tube bending requires a mandrel bender — separate process not currently in system. Flag for manual review.

material_form_type_rules._note: ASTM/AISI spec to form_type mapping. DrawingReaderAgent must assign these correctly.

A36.note: ASTM A36 structural steel — plate or flat bar stock only.

A572_GR50.note: ASTM A572 Gr50 HSLA — plate or flat bar only.

A500_GRB.note: ASTM A500 Gr B is HSS (square/rectangular/round hollow structural section) — always tube.

A1008_CRCS.note: ASTM A1008 cold-rolled carbon steel — sheet metal gauge (<0.125" typically). Laser→brake→weld→finish workflow.

1018_STEEL.note: AISI 1018 cold-drawn — bar stock (flat or round). CNC mill or lathe.

304_STAINLESS.note: 304 SS comes in plate, sheet, and bar forms. Assign form_type from drawing, not material alone.

6061_ALUMINUM.note: 6061 Al in plate, bar, and extrusion forms. Sheet (thin gauge) is 5052 not 6061 typically.

5052_ALUMINUM.note: 5052 Al is the preferred sheet metal alloy — excellent formability, used for bent parts.

6063_ALUMINUM.note: 6063 is an extrusion alloy — structural shapes and extrusions only.

weld_symbol_rules._reference: AWS A2.4-2012 Standard Symbols for Welding

fillet.note: Triangle symbol. Size = leg length. On-arrow-side vs other-side from symbol position.

groove_v.note: V-groove. Requires edge prep (beveling). Adds ~50% time over fillet for same joint length.

groove_bevel.note: Single bevel groove. One side beveled.

groove_u.note: U-groove. Requires machined prep — more time than V.

plug_slot.note: Plug or slot weld. Fill a hole in one plate to join to underlying plate.

spot.note: Resistance spot or arc spot weld.

weld_all_around.note: Circle at joint of arrow and reference line = weld all around (full perimeter).

field_weld.note: Flag = weld in field (not shop). Affects cost assignment (field vs. fabrication).

impossible_combinations: Fillet weld leg size > 0.75× base metal thickness is generally not achievable without distortion
Fillet on 0.048" sheet (16ga) max practical leg = 0.048" (equal thickness)
1" fillet weld on sheet metal is impossible — flag as extraction error

finish_callout_mapping._note: Map common drawing finish callouts to process_id. Used by EngInfoSpecialist to validate finish assignments.

thickness_form_type_plausibility._note: Thickness ranges that are physically plausible per form_type. Flag outliers.

sheet_metal.note: Sheet metal is thin gauge. ≥0.125" is plate, not sheet.

flat_stock.note: Flat bar. Can be thin (1/8" bar) or heavy section.

tube.note: HSS wall thickness. Structural tube is typically 0.065"–0.375" wall.

round_bar.note: Diameter, not thickness. Flag if thickness_in is given for round bar — it's diameter.

A1008_CRCS.note: A1008 comes in 26ga (0.018") to 10ga (0.135"). If thickness > 0.135", likely A36 plate, not A1008 sheet.

A36.note: A36 plate from 3/16" (0.188") standard. Thinner than 3/16" in A36 is rare — may actually be A1008 sheet.

A500_GRB.note: HSS wall thickness 0.065"–0.500" typical. Section size (OD/width) is separate from thickness.
